justification: Pressley's official press release explicitly names both Rep. Pressley and Sen. Markey as co-leads of the reintroduced AI Civil Rights Act (Dec 2025).
Markey's official press release explicitly lists Elizabeth Warren (D-Mass.) as a Senate co-sponsor of the reintroduced AI Civil Rights Act alongside Markey.
Markey's official press release explicitly lists Cory Booker (D-N.J.) as a Senate co-sponsor of the reintroduced AI Civil Rights Act alongside Markey.
Eshoo's official press release explicitly names both Eshoo and Beyer as introducers of the AI Foundation Model Transparency Act.
Lieu's official press release explicitly names Lieu, Eshoo, and Buck as co-introducers of the National AI Commission Act proposing a 20-member commission.
Lieu's official press release explicitly names Eshoo, Buck, and Lieu as co-introducers of the National AI Commission Act proposing a 20-member commission.
Obernolte's official press release confirms the House Task Force on AI was jointly led by Chairman Obernolte and Co-Chair Ted Lieu.
Brown CS news article explicitly names both Suresh Venkatasubramanian and Serena Booth as co-chairs of ACM USTPC's AI and Algorithms Subcommittee.
NY Governor's official announcement explicitly names Hochul and SUNY as partners in the Empire AI campus partnerships for AI research.
City & State NY article explicitly covers both Governor Hochul's FutureWorks Commission and Comptroller DiNapoli's parallel AI workforce efforts in the same piece.
Good Ventures 'About Us' page names Cari Tuna and Dustin Moskovitz as co-founders of Good Ventures, documenting their collaborative philanthropic relationship.
NPR article explicitly covers the NRSC's AI deepfake attack ad targeting Chuck Schumer, naming both parties and documenting Schumer's criticism of the NRSC's use of AI.
Casey Newton's Platformer article 'The Phony Comforts of AI Skepticism' explicitly engages with Gary Marcus's views as representative of AI skepticism, documenting Newton's critique.
Murphy's official press release for the Brookings speech explicitly quotes him calling out Sam Altman by name as 'fundamentally disconnected from reality' and fixated on profit.
Murphy's official Brookings press release explicitly quotes him calling out Mark Zuckerberg by name alongside Sam Altman for dangerous fixation on profit.
